text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, semis and cyclicals led. SOXX ripped +3.94% while SPY and DIA finished only +0.14%, underscoring a duration- and growth-heavy squeeze into AI, small caps, and oil-sensitive cyclicals against a still-wary vol surface and neutral macro tape. Information Technology and Industrials strength tracked the U.S.–Iran peace agreement and a nearly 10% oil drawdown in recent days, while defensives and value factors continued to lag as investors leaned back into growth leadership under a neutral 59.2/100 Fear & Greed backdrop.

INDICES — VIX ticked up to 18.9, modestly above its 20-day moving average of 17.8, signaling slightly elevated downside protection demand. SOXX led majors at +3.94%, followed by IJR +1.28%, IJH +0.92%, QQQ +0.81%, IWM +0.75%, RSP +0.65%, GLD +0.97%, SLV +1.12%, while DIA and SPY lagged at +0.14% with STYLE showing SMALL > LARGE by 0.47 percentage points. The most notable cross-asset move was USO rebounding +2.84% on the day despite being -16.58% over 20 days as the Hormuz reopening and sanctions easing reset crude’s risk premium.

VOLUME — SPY traded at 0.9x average volume and QQQ at 1.2x, with flat SPY price and higher QQQ suggesting low-conviction broad index action but renewed accumulation in growth benchmarks. Elevated 1.5x volume in RSP and 1.2x in IWM versus only 0.8–0.9x in IJH and IJR points to rotation into equal-weight and small caps, while sector flows showed attention on XLRE at 1.1x and XLU at 1.0x and clear de-emphasis in XLC, XLB, and XLE at 0.7–0.8x.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLI Industrials +2.17%, XLV Healthcare +1.49%, XLB Materials +1.33%, XLE Energy +0.97%, XLK Technology +0.83%, XLU Utilities +0.68%, XLRE Real Estate +0.18%, XLF Financials -0.50%, XLP Staples -0.59%, XLC Comms -0.90%, XLY Discretionary -1.49%. Top industries were DRAM +9.95% and MSOS +4.29%, while the weakest were MAGS -2.57% and KWEB -2.76%. Money clearly flowed into semis and AI-adjacent cyclicals while leaking out of China tech and niche security names, consistent with the peace-driven easing in energy risk and better retail data. Net result is early-stage broadening from mega-cap AI into industrial and small-cap growth, so label today as broadening participation.

POSITIONING — SPY’s options showed near-neutral protection with a volume-weighted put-call ratio of 0.98 and modestly positive skew of +6.1%, while QQQ’s 1.18 put-call ratio and +15.6% skew signaled heavier downside hedging in tech. IWM’s elevated put-call ratio of 2.02 and positive skew of +9.3% show substantial protection demand in small caps despite their outperformance, and VIX sitting above its 20-day average confirms residual macro and geopolitical fear rather than outright complacency.

CROSS-ASSET — USO’s +2.84% bounce alongside strong semis and airlines reflects relief that the U.S.–Iran deal cuts tail-risk while leaving enough crude volatility to keep trading interest high. GLD and SLV up +0.97% and +1.12% respectively despite positive equity risk indicates ongoing demand for hedges as rates and the dollar moved only marginally, with UUP down -0.18% and IEF up +0.06%.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 59.2 reads neutral, while MU +16.33% in semis, AMAT about +11.01% in AI equipment, and ARKG +3.97% in genomics were top individual gainers by percentage in their groups. IBUY’s 22.2x volume spike with -1.51% price suggests distribution in e-commerce, whereas IVW’s 4.5x spike with +0.07% flat performance signals quiet accumulation in S&P growth. EWH’s 2.9x volume with +1.53% move highlights renewed interest in Hong Kong equities despite broader China tech weakness. Watch TER (+ strong recent AI-robotics momentum), IYZ (3.2x volume with only +0.14% price showing stealth positioning in telecoms), and IVE (3.2x volume with -0.09% price flagging potential value unwind pressure tomorrow).
